meta.prompt: Initial creation — orientation intro view for documentation workspace

title: What is AppySentinel?
subtitle: A boilerplate for always-on local data coordinators
definition: AppySentinel is a single-host, observer-only, always-on local data coordinator boilerplate. A Sentinel runs on one host — collecting data from local sources and from remote machines via collectors, normalising it into a common envelope, exposing it for queries, and delivering it outward to downstream systems.

core_rules.detail: No UI. Visualisation is a separate Viewer application that consumes the Sentinel through its expose surface. Conflating data coordination with visualisation is the failure mode AppySentinel exists to prevent.
Sentinels read, never mutate the systems they observe. They are first in the boot sequence and last to shut down.
No dashboard or central system is required for a Sentinel to function. It runs and collects whether or not anything is listening.

A Sentinel runs on one host. It can collect data from remote machines via SSH, HTTP, and other protocols — it does not need to be installed on every machine it observes. One Sentinel per machine is an option, not a requirement.

three_umbrellas.name: Collect
three_umbrellas.description: Read data from the outside world — SSH poll, file watch, HTTP poll, SQL diff, subprocess wrap, webhook receiver.

three_umbrellas.name: Expose
three_umbrellas.description: Let the outside world read what the Sentinel knows — via API (HTTP/Hono), CLI (pipe-friendly), or MCP (AI-agent native).

three_umbrellas.name: Deliver
three_umbrellas.description: Push data outward to downstream systems — HTTP push, file relay, Supabase, OTLP, Socket.io.

vs_appystack.appystack: Builds web apps — React + Vite + Express + TypeScript + Socket.io (RVETS). Has a UI by definition.
vs_appystack.appysentinel: Builds always-on local data coordinators — headless, long-running, lightweight. Viewer apps that visualise Sentinel data are built separately, typically on AppyStack.

v1_pilots.name: AppyRadar Sentinel
v1_pilots.description: Fleet dashboard data half — SSH-polls 5 machines, zero remote footprint. Proves the orchestrator-ssh + snapshot-store pattern.

v1_pilots.name: SS Data Query Sentinel
v1_pilots.description: SupportSignal DB mirror — polls Supabase by updated_at watermark, exposes local mirror via MCP for AI agents. Proves the sql-diff-collector + mcp-expose pattern.
